items.title: LIVE: Illinois officials respond to Trump’s comments on military deployment to Chicago
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Illinois Governor JB Pritzker along with State Attorney General Kwame Raoul respond to President Trump’s comments that the U.S. military might deploy to Chicago and is ready to go anywhere on short notice to crack down on crime. Trump said Chicago needs federal help to clean up the city but did not announce a decision.
 
#Live #News #Reuters #Chicago #Trump #JBPritzker #KwameRaoul #Pentagon #Militarytakeover 

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>

items.title: Trump wants to meet North Korea's Kim this year | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump said he wanted to meet with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and that he was open to further trade talks with South Korea even as he lobbed new criticisms at the visiting Asian ally.

#News #USA #Trump #NorthKorea #KimJongUn #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi and law enforcement officials speak after Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada, the alleged Sinaloa cartel co-founder, admitted in a U.S. courtroom to ordering murders and shipping of millions of kilograms of cocaine during his decades-long leadership of the violent Sinaloa cartel.

items.title: DNA sequencing of ancient remains reveals unknown human lineage | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Scientists in Colombia have discovered a previously unknown lineage of human beings after fully sequencing the DNA of ancient remains excavated at archeological sites near the country's capital Bogota.

items.title: Demand for rescued produce grows in NYC amid federal funding cuts
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Because of ongoing federal-funding cuts to food-assistance programs, nonprofits across New York City are seeing a sharp rise in demand for rescued produce.   

At Hunts Point Produce Market in the Bronx, nonprofit Sharing Excess works with wholesalers to recover unsold but perfectly edible fruits and vegetables. Food that would otherwise end up in the trash is redirected to emergency food providers across the city, free of charge.   

#nyc  #funding  #bronx  #foodrescue  #hunger  

👉  Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/reuterssubscribe

items.title: Trump says military 'may or may not' deploy to Chicago
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>President Trump said the U.S. military might deploy to Chicago and is ready to go anywhere on short notice to crack down on crime. Trump said Chicago needs federal help, but did not announce a decision.
 
Trump has seized control of the police force in Washington, D.C., and is allowing National Guard troops to carry weapons while on patrol in the city. Chicago has long had high levels of gun violence, but crime — including homicide — has declined in the last year. 
  
#trump #military #troops #nationalguard #chicago

items.title: Gamble in Texas pays off for Chinese copper company | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Chinese copper wire manufacturer Wellascent’s decision last year to build a factory in Texas was partly a hedge against geopolitical risk. Now, amid a US-China trade war, the wager appears to be paying off.
 
#News #Reuters #Newsfeed #tariffs #tariffwar #tradewar

items.title: US consumers with prime credit are slipping up on payments | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>US consumers with the highest credit scores are starting to fall behind in debt repayments, credit scoring company VantageScore said in a report, in a sign that Americans' financial health may be deteriorating more broadly than expected.

items.title: Ugandan youth turn slums into sustainable urban farm | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>In Kamwokya, an informal settlement in Kampala, Uganda, young people are learning to grow food in tight spaces, using containers and vertical gardens, as part of a community-led urban-farming initiative.

#News #Uganda #Kampala #Farming #Sustainability #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.title: LIVE: Trump meets with South Korea's new President Lee
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>U.S. President Donald Trump meets with South Korean President Lee Jae Myung in the Oval Office, just hours after Trump launched criticism at the new president as conflict over defense spending and trade tests the two countries' decades-old alliance.
 
#trump #southkorea #trade #defense #leejaemyung

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>

items.title: LIVE: Sinaloa cartel’s Ismael Zambada in Brooklyn for plea hearing
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada, the alleged Sinaloa cartel co-founder facing U.S. drug-trafficking charges, will appear in federal court in Brooklyn for a change-of-plea hearing before U.S. District Judge Brian Cogan. He had previously pleaded not guilty to charges stemming from his decades allegedly leading the Sinaloa cartel alongside imprisoned kingpin Joaquin "El Chapo" Guzman.
 
#Live #News #Reuters #Mexico #US #Sinaloacartel #IsmaelZambada #ElChapo #JoaquinGuzman

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>

items.title: LIVE: Trump signs executive orders
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>U.S. President Trump is expected to sign several executive orders, including one that directs the U.S. attorney general to prosecute those who desecrate the American flag, including by burning, according to a fact sheet from the White House.
 
#trump #washington #usa #politics #executiveorder

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>
